---
title: "Phyllanthus Niruri"
slug: phyllanthus-niruri
category: "Fruits, Veg & Botanicals"
category-slug: fruits-veg-botanicals
keywords: ["Ayurveda", "Bhumi Amla", "Tamalaki", "Herbal extract", "Liver health", "Traditional medicine", "Nutraceutical"]
description: "Phyllanthus Niruri, known in India as Bhumi Amla or Tamalaki, is an annual herbaceous plant deeply rooted in traditional Indian medicine, especially Ayurveda"
---

## History and Sourcing

[Phyllanthus Niruri](phyllanthus-niruri.qmd), known in India as Bhumi [Amla](amla.qmd) or Tamalaki, is an annual herbaceous plant deeply rooted in traditional Indian medicine, especially Ayurveda. Its name, 'Bhumi Amla' (earth gooseberry), refers to its small, berry-like fruits. Native to tropical and subtropical regions, it grows wild across India and has been used for millennia, documented in ancient Ayurvedic texts for its therapeutic properties.

## Home Kitchen Use

While not a conventional culinary ingredient due to its intensely bitter taste, [Phyllanthus Niruri](phyllanthus-niruri.qmd) is widely used in traditional Indian home remedies. The entire plant is prepared as decoctions (kadha), juices, or pastes to support liver health, aid digestion, and act as a general tonic. Fresh preparations are often preferred in traditional medicine to maximize the potency of its compounds.

## Industrial Applications

Industrially, [Phyllanthus Niruri](phyllanthus-niruri.qmd) is primarily utilized as F-EXTRACT in nutraceuticals, herbal supplements, and standardized Ayurvedic formulations. These extracts concentrate bioactive compounds like lignans, flavonoids, tannins, and alkaloids, which are associated with its hepatoprotective, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory effects. The extract form enables precise dosing and easy integration into various health products for the FMCG and pharmaceutical industries.

## Distinctions and Common Confusion

A frequent confusion stems from its common name, 'Bhumi [Amla](amla.qmd),' leading to its mistaken identity with *Emblica officinalis* (true Amla or Indian Gooseberry). Despite some shared traditional uses, *Phyllanthus niruri* is a distinct plant with unique botanical characteristics and specific therapeutic benefits, particularly for liver support. Additionally, other *Phyllanthus* species like *P. amarus* and *P. fraternus* are often used interchangeably, adding to the complexity of its identification.
